name: Zerodha-Style Trading Platform — Two-Service Architecture
scenario: Build two collaborating microservices for a stock trading platform: market-service (provides stock prices and availability) and order-service (places buy/sell orders, calling market-service synchronously for price validation). Each is a separate Spring Boot application with its own database.
description: Two independent Spring Boot applications. market-service (port 8081): stocks with current prices. order-service (port 8082): calls market-service via RestTemplate before placing orders. Each with its own H2 database. MockRestServiceServer tests covering success and error scenarios.

core.description: `market-service` (port 8081, spring.application.name=market-service): Stock @Entity (symbol, companyName, currentPrice, availableShares). StockRepository. StockController: GET /api/stocks/{symbol}, GET /api/stocks/{symbol}/quote (returns {symbol, price, availableShares, tradeable}).
`order-service` (port 8082, spring.application.name=order-service): TradeOrder @Entity (id, userId, symbol, quantity, price, type BUY/SELL, status). TradeOrderRepository. RestTemplate @Bean.

`TradeService.executeTrade(userId, symbol, quantity, type)`: calls market-service GET /api/stocks/{symbol}/quote (RestTemplate). Validates: stock tradeable, sufficient shares for BUY. Saves order with current price. Returns the saved order.
`TradeController` (order-service): POST /api/trades {userId, symbol, quantity, type} → 201 with order, 400/409 on validation failure.
@SpringBootTest tests with MockRestServiceServer (5 tests): successful BUY, successful SELL, stock not tradeable (reject), insufficient shares (reject), market-service down (RuntimeException).

bonus.description: Add a @CommandLineRunner to market-service that seeds 5 real NSE stocks (TCS, RELIANCE, INFY, HDFC, ICICI) with realistic prices. Add GET /api/stocks to list all.
Add transaction history: after a successful trade, order-service calls a (mocked) portfolio-service to update holdings. Mock this call in tests.

Add inter-service authentication: RestTemplate @Bean in order-service is configured with a ClientHttpRequestInterceptor that adds X-Service-Name=order-service header to all outgoing calls. market-service validates this header.

data_to_use:
market-service seed data:
TCS:       ₹3890, 1000000 shares, tradeable=true
RELIANCE:  ₹2450, 2000000 shares, tradeable=true
INFY:      ₹1560, 1500000 shares, tradeable=true
HDFC:      ₹1670, 800000 shares,  tradeable=true
PAYTM:     ₹475,  500000 shares,  tradeable=false (suspended)

Test scenarios:
BUY 10 TCS at ₹3890 → success
SELL 5 RELIANCE → success (no share count check on SELL)
BUY 10 PAYTM → reject (tradeable=false)
BUY 2000000 HDFC → reject (insufficient shares)
BUY TCS (market-service down) → RuntimeException
ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. Two separate Spring Boot apps, different ports. market-service /quote: returns {symbol, price, availableShares, tradeable}. MockRestServiceServer: server.expect(requestTo(url)).andRespond(withSuccess(json, APPLICATION_JSON)). withException(new IOException()) for service-down test. @BeforeEach server = MockRestServiceServer.createServer(restTemplate).
submission_instructions: Two separate GitHub repos (or subdirectories). Both Spring Boot apps run simultaneously. README shows how to start both. All 5 tests pass.
tips_for_success: Two pom.xml files, two Application.java classes, two application.properties — truly separate
market-service quote endpoint: map Stock entity to a QuoteDto (don't return @Entity directly to other services)
TradeService: catch HttpClientErrorException.NotFound for unknown symbol
Test: server.expect() calls must match the ORDER of RestTemplate calls in the service method
withException(new IOException('Connection refused')): simulates network failure
concepts_reinforced: Each service has its own database
MockRestServiceServer for testing without real HTTP
Synchronous REST for critical path validation
